intended_use: Ground FHIR-like resource shape checks
Explain profile and search-parameter requirements
Provide interoperability evidence for review-gated mappings
prohibited_use: Do not claim full FHIR compliance without a real FHIR validator
Do not infer clinical meaning beyond source fields
license_constraints: Respect HL7 publication terms and cite exact release/version
policy_notes: Use source version in evidence when possible.

policies.source_name: Unified Code for Units of Measure
intended_use: Ground missing or ambiguous unit warnings
Support review-gated unit normalization candidates
prohibited_use: Do not silently convert units without preserving original value and review state
license_constraints: Use UCUM terms as published; keep validation adapters versioned
policy_notes: Unit normalization changes must stay review-gated.

intended_use: Generate candidate lab-code evidence
Support reviewer decisions for lab-name normalization
prohibited_use: Do not bulk-ingest licensed files into git
Do not auto-code lab names without review
license_constraints: Full downloads and terminology service access may require account/license terms
policy_notes: Keep selected public pages separate from licensed bulk assets.

intended_use: Support medication terminology lookup and source-linked reviewer candidates
prohibited_use: Do not make medication safety recommendations without clinical review
license_constraints: Cache API results with source release metadata
policy_notes: Drug normalization remains a reviewer decision.

intended_use: Support review-gated clinical concept candidates
Ground Graph-NER entities and FHIR-like Condition or AllergyIntolerance mappings
prohibited_use: Do not store or distribute licensed SNOMED CT release content without verifying deployment rights
Do not auto-code diagnoses or findings without licensed lookup and clinical review
license_constraints: SNOMED CT access and use depend on national member/license terms
Preserve source URL, version, and reviewer decision for any terminology candidate
policy_notes: The current implementation may emit placeholder candidates only; production coding requires licensed terminology services.

policies.authority: CDC National Center for Health Statistics
intended_use: Route diagnosis-code evidence to human coders or reviewers
Ground analytics export warnings for diagnosis classification
prohibited_use: Do not use retrieval evidence as automatic billing code assignment
Do not infer clinical diagnosis from code candidates alone
license_constraints: Use official CDC/NCHS release metadata and preserve version year
policy_notes: Final code assignment remains a coder or clinician decision.

intended_use: Ground analytics export previews and unmapped-field warnings
Explain why source provenance and terminology coverage are required before OMOP export
prohibited_use: Do not represent preview exports as production CDM loads without validation
Do not drop source provenance or review metadata during analytics mapping
license_constraints: Preserve OMOP CDM version and vocabulary release context
policy_notes: OMOP is analytics context, not clinical decision support.

intended_use: Support transparent literature-search query expansion
Explain MeSH/text-word search boundaries before external PubMed handoff
prohibited_use: Do not treat literature-search routing as clinical recommendation
Do not export PHI or patient identifiers into public literature search queries
license_constraints: Preserve MeSH release year and NLM source URL when cached
policy_notes: Use MeSH to improve search transparency, not to make clinical claims.

policies.authority: U.S. National Library of Medicine / NCBI
intended_use: Generate transparent PubMed follow-up searches
Cache article metadata and abstracts where allowed
Support evidence-grounded explanations with citation provenance
prohibited_use: Do not export PHI or patient identifiers to PubMed queries
Do not treat literature retrieval as diagnosis, treatment, or clinical recommendation
Do not bypass publisher full-text license boundaries
license_constraints: Respect NCBI usage guidelines and publisher source terms
Preserve PMID, DOI/source URL, query hash, fetch time, and abstract availability
policy_notes: Search details, MeSH translations, and query strings must remain visible to reviewers.
Literature context can support review, but cannot replace clinical judgment.

intended_use: Route transparent trial registry searches
Cache NCT-linked study summaries and eligibility text
Support research-context retrieval with source timestamps
prohibited_use: Do not present trial search as patient-specific enrollment guidance
Do not infer treatment suitability from registry metadata alone
license_constraints: Preserve NCT ID, API endpoint, query, fetch time, and data timestamp when available
policy_notes: Trial status and eligibility text can change; use fresh source timestamps in evidence.
Trial records are research context, not clinical decision support.

intended_use: Route regulatory searches and cite public FDA datasets as context
prohibited_use: Do not treat adverse event counts as causal clinical conclusions
license_constraints: Public data; preserve endpoint, query, and fetch time
policy_notes: Regulatory context is not clinical decision support.
